findings.reasoning: I found no source-level storage-layout drift or packed-slot write that crosses into another Game field. All modules inherit the shared storage base directly or through utility bases: DegenerusGameStorage is the common base at contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:120, Advance at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:30, Lootbox at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:39, Boon at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameBoonModule.sol:22, PayoutUtils at contracts/modules/DegenerusGamePayoutUtils.sol:8, MintStreakUtils at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameMintStreakUtils.sol:20. The critical packed writers are mask-preserving: slot 7 balance halves use explicit low/high-half helpers at contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:923 and contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:927, with checked debit/credit at contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:942, contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:950, and contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:967. Slot 9 BitPackingLib shifts match the packed layout constants at contracts/libraries/BitPackingLib.sol:52 through contracts/libraries/BitPackingLib.sol:88 and setPacked masks before write at contracts/libraries/BitPackingLib.sol:104. Slot 34 Lootbox RNG fields are defined and masked at contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:1537, with preserving writers in Advance, Mint, and Afking at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1656, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameMintModule.sol:1609, and contracts/modules/GameAfkingModule.sol:1039. Slot 40 uses explicit window masks at contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:1708 and contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:1725. Slot 51 Boon fields use field masks at contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:1955 and contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:1985, and Boon/Lootbox writes are targeted at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameBoonModule.sol:68 and contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:1726. I could not run forge inspect in this read-only sandbox against the frozen tree, so this verdict is based on the checked source and provided layout map rather than compiler-emitted layouts.

The nested payable delegatecall paths do preserve msg.value, but the payable nested bodies either ignore msg.value or account it once through explicit amount parameters. Boon purchase/activity/expiry handlers are payable-tolerant but do not credit or spend msg.value: contracts/modules/DegenerusGameBoonModule.sol:68, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameBoonModule.sol:125, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameBoonModule.sol:288. Mint splits fresh ETH explicitly before nested calls at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameMintModule.sol:1402 and records payment through explicit values at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameMintModule.sol:2097; _processMintPayment consumes the explicit ethForLeg and balance buckets once at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameMintModule.sol:235. Lootbox redemption paths require the authorized SDGNRS caller, cap msg.value against amount, and settle only the explicit amount at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:910 and contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:995. resolveLootboxDirect is payable only to tolerate in-flight value and resolves from the explicit amount parameter at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:861. subscribe validates consent/funding and credits msg.value once at contracts/modules/GameAfkingModule.sol:308 and contracts/modules/GameAfkingModule.sol:322. I found no reachable double-spend or stranded ETH in the Game path.

The raw delegatecall(msg.data) dispatches use fixed constants, not caller-chosen targets. consumeCoinflipBoon checks msg.sender is COIN or COINFLIP and delegates only to GAME_BOON_MODULE at contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:827. Afking router stubs delegate only to GAME_AFKING_MODULE at contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:357, contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:376, contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:389, contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:402, contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:416, contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:428, and contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:439. Module addresses are nonzero fixed constants at contracts/ContractAddresses.sol:13 through contracts/ContractAddresses.sol:36. Return data is either bubbled through _revertDelegate at contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:945 or length-checked before decoding, e.g. contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:279, contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:827, and contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:402. Selector checks with cast found no collision among the forwarded Boon and Afking selectors.

I found no swallowed module failure that lets the caller assume a successful transition after partial state. The Game-level delegatecall reverter bubbles original return data at contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:945. Advance has its own bubbling helper at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:808 and uses it on nested Jackpot, Afking, Mint, and GameOver calls including contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:771, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:795, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1016, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1473, and contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1541. Decimator, Degenerette, and Afking nested Lootbox calls bubble via their helpers at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDecimatorModule.sol:669, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDegeneretteModule.sol:924, and contracts/modules/GameAfkingModule.sol:1476. Lootbox nested Boon/Degenerette calls use generic reverts rather than preserving the original bytes, but still revert atomically at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:1281, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:1418, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:2098, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:2117, and contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:2136. The one swallow in _handleGameOverPath is explicit and bounded: it treats Mint drain failure as non-fatal only to continue final GameOver cleanup at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:658 and contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:688.

findings.trigger: Call the deployed GAME_BOON_MODULE directly, e.g. consumePurchaseBoost{value: 1 wei}(victimOrSelf) when module-local boonPacked is empty. The call returns 0 and leaves the caller's ETH in the Boon module contract balance, not in Game accounting.
findings.reasoning: The delegatecall column itself is not hijackable: module constants are fixed and nonzero at contracts/ContractAddresses.sol:13 through contracts/ContractAddresses.sol:36, and Game stubs delegate only to those constants. However, the literal claim that no module is reachable by direct external call is false. BoonModule exposes payable external functions with no address(this)==GAME direct-call guard and no msg.value accounting, including contracts/modules/DegenerusGameBoonModule.sol:68, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameBoonModule.sol:125, and contracts/modules/DegenerusGameBoonModule.sol:288. Direct calls execute against module-local storage, so they do not corrupt Game storage or drain Game funds. Most asset-touching direct module paths are further contained by external token/game authorization, e.g. FLIP onlyGame gates at contracts/FLIP.sol:475 and contracts/FLIP.sol:593. This is a user-fund sink / hygiene issue, not a protocol-column integrity break under honest governance.

The Lootbox -> Degenerette -> Lootbox recirculation does not form an unbounded ETH-spin cascade. Lootbox calls EthSpin only when allowEthSpin is true and otherwise awards tickets at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:2026. resolveLootboxDirect passes allowEthSpin=false at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:889, so the recursive call from Degenerette terminates in the non-ETH-spin branch. Degenerette box spin entrypoints also require delegatecall context by checking address(this)==GAME at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDegeneretteModule.sol:1402, and its recirculation uses the fixed Lootbox module and bubbles failure at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDegeneretteModule.sol:917 and contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDegeneretteModule.sol:924.

findings.lead: Hotspot: msg.value in flight at depth
findings.reasoning: Preserved msg.value is accounted through explicit amount variables, not implicitly re-spent at depth. The main fresh ETH split happens in Mint at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameMintModule.sol:1862 and contracts/modules/DegenerusGameMintModule.sol:1913; nested Boon calls ignore msg.value at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameBoonModule.sol:68 and contracts/modules/DegenerusGameBoonModule.sol:288. Lootbox redemption caps msg.value and settles explicit amount at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:910 and contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:995. Afking subscription credits msg.value once after its gates at contracts/modules/GameAfkingModule.sol:308. I found no depth-dependent stale-value or double-counted-value path.

The high-risk packed slots remain mask/half guarded across module writers. balancesPacked uses low/high 128-bit helpers and checked combined debit at contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:923, contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:927, and contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:967. lootboxRngPacked defines index, pending box, pending ETH, timestamp, and active request masks at contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:1537, with preserving writers in Advance/Mint/Afking at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1656, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameMintModule.sol:1609, and contracts/modules/GameAfkingModule.sol:1039. Slot 5 fields totalFlipReversals and lastVrfProcessedTimestamp are declared separately in the shared layout at contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:399 and contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:405; I found no raw unmasked write to that packed slot. The only raw sstore found in modules is ticket-array maintenance in Mint at contracts/modules/DegenerusGameMintModule.sol:781, not one of the shared packed slots.

overallVerdict: No catastrophic or high-severity delegatecall-integrity counterexample was found for the Game column. DELEGATE-01 through DELEGATE-04 and the named hotspots are refuted as exploitable against Game storage or accounting. The only concrete counterexample is LOW severity: direct calls to payable Boon module functions can strand the caller's ETH in module-local storage/balance, but this does not corrupt Game storage, does not select a delegatecall target, and does not drain protocol funds under honest governance.
